title: Schema files duplicated across references/schemas/ and schemas/ directories
summary: All 5 JSON schema files exist identically in both packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/ and packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/. This creates a maintenance risk where one copy could be updated without the other.
detail_html:
Five schema files (ArchitectureAssessmentOutput, ConceptUpdate, FileReviewOutcome, ReviewConcept, SemanticOutput) are byte-identical copies in two directories. The references/schemas/ copy is referenced by agent paradigm prompts; the schemas/ copy appears to serve models.py and the assembler. If only one copy is updated in a future PR, schema divergence will cause silent validation mismatches.

Recommendation: Pick one canonical location and symlink or remove the other. Alternatively, have the Pydantic models in models.py export the schemas programmatically.

title: Well-structured 4-phase pipeline with clear component boundaries
summary: The PR establishes a clean 4-phase architecture (Setup -> Review -> Assemble -> Deliver) with proper separation of concerns: models.py owns validation, assemble_review_pack.py owns transformation, render_review_pack.py owns presentation, and agent definitions are decoupled from paradigm prompts.
detail_html:
The pipeline decomposition is architecturally sound:

  • Phase 1 (Setup): review_pack_setup.py orchestrates checkout, diff generation, scaffolding, and JSONL pre-creation.
  • Phase 2 (Review): 6 independent agents write JSONL files, each with a FileReviewOutcome per file plus ReviewConcept findings. Agent identity derived from filename, not data — clean design.
  • Phase 3 (Assemble): assemble_review_pack.py validates and transforms JSONL into ReviewPackData. models.py provides Pydantic validation as the enforcement layer.
  • Phase 4 (Deliver): render_review_pack.py produces the HTML artifact, with Playwright e2e tests verifying the output.

The separation between agent definitions (agents/) and paradigm prompts (packages/review-prompts/) correctly decouples agent identity/tooling from review methodology.

title: Cross-zone coupling between review-pack and review-prompts is intentional and well-bounded
summary: The review-pack zone's agent definitions reference paradigm prompts in the review-prompts zone via path variables. This coupling is deliberate — agents are the harness, prompts are the methodology. Both zones are updated in sync in this PR.
detail_html:
Agent definitions in packages/pr-review-pack/agents/ reference prompts via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/review-prompts/. The review-prompts zone is independently versioned and separately maintained. This PR updates both zones coherently: agent definitions point to the updated prompt format, and prompts now specify JSONL output with FileReviewOutcome requirements.

The coupling is narrow (path reference only) and the contract is clear (JSONL schema). This is healthy cross-zone interaction.

locations.comment: Updated paradigm prompt matching new agent contract

title: docs/research/ subdirectory not explicitly covered in zone registry
summary: New docs/research/ directory is implicitly covered by the docs zone's docs/*.md glob (Python fnmatch matches through path separators), but this is fragile — a switch to pathlib glob or POSIX glob would break the match.
detail_html:
The file docs/research/anthropic_skill_architecture.md is matched by the docs/*.md pattern only because Python's fnmatch treats * as matching /. Standard POSIX glob and pathlib.Path.match() do NOT match / with *. If the zone-matching implementation changes, this file would become unzoned.

Recommendation: Add docs/research/** to the docs zone paths, or to repo-infra alongside docs/proposals/**.

locations.comment: Implicitly zoned via docs/*.md glob — fragile match

registryWarnings.warning: docs/research/** not explicitly covered — relies on fnmatch * matching / which is non-standard glob behavior

couplingWarnings.evidence: Agent definitions reference paradigm prompts via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/review-prompts/ path. Coupling is intentional and well-bounded through JSONL schema contract.

summary: This PR is a major but architecturally clean evolution of the review-pack system from a 3-pass to a 4-phase pipeline. The new architecture introduces proper separation of concerns: Pydantic models for validation, an assembler for transformation, independent agent definitions, and a structured JSONL contract between review agents and the assembler. Cross-zone coupling between review-pack and review-prompts is intentional and narrow. The only structural concern is duplicated schema files across two directories (references/schemas/ and schemas/) which creates a future maintenance risk. All files are zoned, though docs/research/ coverage depends on fnmatch glob behavior.
